query: I'm comparing 4 candidate models for our binary classifier — Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM. I ran 10-fold cross-validation on each, so I have a per-fold accuracy array of length 10 for each model. All four use the same 10 fold splits. Which model is the winner, and which differences are real?
expected_behavior: Identifies that there are 4 models → 6 pairwise comparisons (k * (k - 1) / 2) and requires multiple-comparison correction (Bonferroni, Holm-Bonferroni, OR the Friedman + Nemenyi framework) BEFORE declaring any pair significant — does not just look at raw p-values across the 6 pairs

Recommends a PAIRED per-fold test (paired t-test if per-fold differences are roughly Normal, OR Wilcoxon signed-rank if not; Demsar 2006 recommends Wilcoxon as the default), NOT an unpaired t-test on the per-fold metric distributions

Either explicitly names Holm-Bonferroni / Bonferroni for the pairwise correction, OR names the Friedman omnibus test followed by Nemenyi post-hoc as the Demsar 2006 framework for comparing k models on N folds
